NAME: Divay Aggarwal
SUMMARY: Results-driven Software Engineer with expertise in systems development using \textbf{C++ programming} and \textbf{Python scripting}. Proven experience in the \textbf{Platform} domain, building scalable \textbf{Linux systems} and infrastructure-level software components handling production diagnostics. Proficient in \textbf{Systems Programming}, \textbf{Operating Systems}, and \textbf{Bash scripting automation}. Passionate about debugging complex issues through \textbf{Test-Driven Development (TDD)} and applying OS fundamentals to solve concurrency and performance challenges.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Spearheading the system-level architecture of \textbf{"Redbooks"}, a mission-critical financial ledger platform facilitating automated accounting and auditing for over \textbf{\$5M+ in monthly transactions}.

Engineered complex \textbf{Python modules} to encapsulate "posting criteria logic," automating the generation of \textbf{AR/AP Invoices, Journal Entries, and Credit Memos} with strict \textbf{data consistency guarantees}.
Designed resilient \textbf{data pipelines} using \textbf{Python automation tools} to synchronize \textbf{50,000+ daily records} from distributed systems to the Redbooks, ensuring reliable processing.
Optimized \textbf{PostgreSQL} performance via compound indexing and query refactoring, reducing average query execution time by \textbf{35\%} under high system load.
Championed \textbf{Test-Driven Development (TDD)} by implementing comprehensive \textbf{pytest} test suites, achieving \textbf{90\% code coverage} and improving debugging efficiency.

experience.title: Software Engineer Intern
bullets: Architected a \textbf{Linux-based system utility} for budget analysis, devising a custom \textbf{Weighted Moving Average (WMA)} forecasting formula to predict expected billing with \textbf{92\% accuracy}.
Engineered granular \textbf{Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)}, defining distinct dashboard views and permissions for \textbf{Super Admins, CEOs, and Managers} to monitor system metrics.
Implemented \textbf{system audit logs} for debugging and integrated \textbf{automated alerts} via \textbf{Python} to notify stakeholders of anomalies, reducing excess spend by 15\%.
Designed interactive \textbf{system dashboards} to display real-time usage trends, optimizing \textbf{data structure} aggregations to handle complex system data efficiently.

projects.title: Linux Storage Analyzer (Filesystem Debugging Tool)
projects.tech: C++, Python, Linux OS, Bash, ext4
bullets: Developed a systems-level storage analyzer using \textbf{C++}, interacting with \textbf{Linux kernel interfaces} to monitor filesystem behavior and detect inconsistencies in storage layers.
Built an \textbf{automated debugging framework} using \textbf{Python} and \textbf{Bash}, reducing system-level issue diagnosis time by \textbf{40\%} across multiple environments.
Implemented permission-aware modules using \textbf{Linux} and \textbf{ext4} semantics, ensuring reliable data handling and isolation across system components.

projects.title: Process Monitor (Linux Systems Performance Tool)
projects.tech: Python, C++, Linux OS, Bash, pytest
bullets: Engineered a high-concurrency \textbf{Linux systems tool} to monitor OS processes, using \textbf{Python} scripts and \textbf{Bash} to track and analyze up to 1,000 concurrent processes.
Optimized \textbf{process scheduling insights} using \textbf{caching strategies} and enforced system stability through efficient \textbf{C++} data structures.
